section_name: Crime classification for violation of ORS 811.705
chapter_title: Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
text:
The Oregon Criminal Justice Commission shall classify the crime of failure to perform the duties of a driver to injured persons as crime category 8 of the sentencing guidelines grid of the commission if a person suffers serious physical injury as defined in ORS 161.015 (General definitions) or dies as a result of the collision that forms the basis of the conviction. [2001 c.919 §2; 2018 c.22 §3]

Note: 811.707 (Crime classification for violation of ORS 811.705) was enacted into law by the Legislative Assembly but was not added to or made a part of ORS chapter 811 or any series therein by legislative action. See Preface to Oregon Revised Statutes for further explanation.
markdown:
## Title 59
### Chapter 811. Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
##### § 811.707 — Crime classification for violation of ORS 811.705

The Oregon Criminal Justice Commission shall classify the crime of failure to perform the duties of a driver to injured persons as crime category 8 of the sentencing guidelines grid of the commission if a person suffers serious physical injury as defined in ORS 161.015 (General definitions) or dies as a result of the collision that forms the basis of the conviction. [2001 c.919 §2; 2018 c.22 §3]

Note: 811.707 (Crime classification for violation of ORS 811.705) was enacted into law by the Legislative Assembly but was not added to or made a part of ORS chapter 811 or any series therein by legislative action. See Preface to Oregon Revised Statutes for further explanation.
